task_description:
Screen these abstracts for a systematic review on ML methods for automated clinical diagnosis of cardiac conditions. Produce a ranked top-5 shortlist of the most relevant and methodologically rigorous papers.

INCLUDE only papers that present or evaluate an ML model applied directly to automated cardiac diagnosis (arrhythmia detection, heart failure classification, valve disease detection, coronary artery disease detection). Prognosis, treatment selection, cardiac rehabilitation, surgical planning, and imaging reconstruction should be EXCLUDE.

For each INCLUDE paper: assign rank 1 (best) to 5 (fifth-best) and write one justification sentence (max 200 chars).

Quality rubric: 1=no method; 2=vague method; 3=clear method with metrics; 4=full method with baselines and ablation.

papers.title: Gradient Boosting for 10-Year MACE Prognosis in Stable Coronary Disease
papers.abstract: We train gradient boosting on 28,000 stable coronary disease patients from the PROMISE trial to predict 10-year major adverse cardiovascular events. C-index 0.79 is achieved. This is a prognostic model; patients already have confirmed coronary disease and the model predicts future events, not disease presence.
papers.topic_hint: gradient boosting, MACE prognosis, coronary disease, PROMISE trial
papers.methodology_hint: gradient boosting, C-index 0.79, prognosis not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: Gradient boosting for MACE prognosis C-index 0.79, prognostic not diagnostic.

papers.title: Transformer for 20-Class Holter Arrhythmia Diagnosis from 48-Hour ECG Recordings
papers.abstract: We train a transformer on 75,000 48-hour Holter ECG recordings from four hospital systems for 20-class arrhythmia diagnosis using multi-label learning. Macro-AUC 0.94 is achieved with prospective blinded cardiologist reader study validation. Per-class ablation of the attention temporal receptive field is provided alongside public code release.
papers.topic_hint: transformer, Holter ECG, arrhythmia diagnosis, multi-label, 20-class
papers.methodology_hint: transformer, 75k 48-hour Holters, macro-AUC 0.94, prospective reader study, attention ablation, code
papers.claimed_contribution: Transformer for 20-class Holter arrhythmia diagnosis macro-AUC 0.94, prospective validation, code released.

papers.title: Deep RL for Personalised Cardiac Rehabilitation Exercise Intensity Prescription
papers.abstract: Cardiac rehabilitation outcomes depend on exercise intensity personalisation. We apply reinforcement learning to optimise exercise prescription in 1,200 post-MI rehabilitation patients. The RL policy achieves 23% greater VO2 max improvement versus fixed protocols. This is treatment personalisation, not cardiac diagnosis.
papers.topic_hint: reinforcement learning, cardiac rehabilitation, exercise prescription, post-MI
papers.methodology_hint: RL, rehabilitation, VO2 max improvement — treatment not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: RL cardiac rehabilitation prescription improving VO2 max 23%, treatment not diagnosis.

papers.title: 3D CNN for Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy Diagnosis and Differential from Cardiac MRI
papers.abstract: We train a 3D CNN on 5,200 cardiac MRI cine stacks from UK Biobank and three clinical centres to diagnose hypertrophic cardiomyopathy and distinguish it from athlete's heart and hypertensive cardiomyopathy. AUC 0.97 for HCM detection and macro-AUC 0.93 for the three-way differential are achieved, outperforming CMR-reporting cardiologists. Full temporal and structural feature ablation is provided.
papers.topic_hint: 3D CNN, hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, cardiac MRI, differential diagnosis
papers.methodology_hint: 3D CNN, 5200 CMR stacks, AUC 0.97, macro-AUC 0.93, cardiologist comparison, ablation
papers.claimed_contribution: 3D CNN for HCM differential diagnosis AUC 0.97, outperforming CMR cardiologists, full ablation.

papers.title: GAN for Cardiac MRI Super-Resolution and Motion Correction
papers.abstract: Motion artefacts and low resolution degrade cardiac MRI quality. We train a conditional GAN for simultaneous super-resolution and motion correction on 8,000 cardiac MRI studies. SSIM improves from 0.71 to 0.89. This is an image quality enhancement method; no cardiac condition is diagnosed.
papers.topic_hint: GAN, cardiac MRI, super-resolution, motion correction, image quality
papers.methodology_hint: conditional GAN, cardiac MRI, SSIM 0.89 — image quality not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: GAN for CMR super-resolution and motion correction SSIM 0.89, not diagnostic.

papers.title: Attention RNN for Atrial Fibrillation Detection from Single-Lead PPG Wristband
papers.abstract: We train an attention-based RNN on 22,000 30-second PPG segments from wristband devices to detect paroxysmal AF. AUC 0.96 and sensitivity 0.94 at specificity 0.95 are achieved, validated against 12-lead ECG gold standard. Attention weights correctly localise RR interval irregularity. Inter-patient and intra-patient evaluation strategies are both reported.
papers.topic_hint: attention RNN, PPG, atrial fibrillation, wristband, AF detection
papers.methodology_hint: attention RNN, 22k PPG segments, AUC 0.96, ECG gold standard, attention localisation, dual evaluation
papers.claimed_contribution: Attention RNN for AF detection from PPG, AUC 0.96, ECG-validated, attention localisation.

papers.title: Bayesian NN for Uncertainty-Aware Heart Failure Mortality Prognosis
papers.abstract: Heart failure prognosis with calibrated uncertainty is clinically important. We train a Bayesian NN on 35,000 heart failure patients from a multi-centre registry to predict 1-year mortality. C-index 0.81 is achieved. This predicts survival probability in confirmed heart failure patients, not the diagnosis itself.
papers.topic_hint: Bayesian NN, heart failure prognosis, mortality, uncertainty
papers.methodology_hint: Bayesian NN, C-index 0.81, heart failure prognosis — survival prediction not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: Bayesian NN for heart failure mortality prognosis C-index 0.81, prognostic not diagnostic.

papers.title: ViT for Six-Class Valvular Heart Disease Diagnosis from Echocardiography Videos
papers.abstract: We fine-tune a ViT-B/16 on 42,000 echocardiographic cine clips for six-class valvular diagnosis including aortic stenosis, mitral regurgitation, and tricuspid regurgitation across severity grades. Macro-AUC 0.95 is achieved with validation against a board-certified echocardiographer consensus. Temporal attention maps highlight peak systole and diastole as the most informative frames.
papers.topic_hint: ViT, echocardiography, valvular heart disease, diagnosis, aortic stenosis
papers.methodology_hint: ViT-B/16, 42k echo videos, macro-AUC 0.95, echocardiographer consensus, temporal attention maps
papers.claimed_contribution: ViT for six-class valvular diagnosis macro-AUC 0.95, echocardiographer-validated, temporal attention.

papers.title: ResNet-50 for Structural Cardiac Abnormality Detection from Frontal Chest X-Ray
papers.abstract: We train ResNet-50 on 110,000 frontal CXRs to detect six structural cardiac findings including cardiomegaly, pericardial effusion, and aortic root dilatation. Mean AUC 0.92 is achieved, outperforming five radiology resident readers, with external validation on two independent health systems.
papers.topic_hint: ResNet-50, chest X-ray, cardiac abnormality, cardiomegaly, detection
papers.methodology_hint: ResNet-50, 110k CXRs, mean AUC 0.92, 5 radiology residents, two external validations
papers.claimed_contribution: ResNet-50 for cardiac abnormality detection from CXR, AUC 0.92, two external validations.

papers.title: Physics-Informed NN for Patient-Specific Haemodynamic Simulation
papers.abstract: We develop a physics-informed NN embedding Navier-Stokes equations for patient-specific coronary haemodynamics simulation. Results support fractional flow reserve estimation without invasive catheterisation. This is computational simulation for procedural planning, not automated cardiac diagnosis from patient data.
papers.topic_hint: physics-informed NN, haemodynamics, FFR, coronary, simulation
papers.methodology_hint: PINN, Navier-Stokes, FFR simulation — computational simulation not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: PINN for FFR simulation supporting procedural planning, not automated diagnosis.

papers.title: XGBoost for Coronary Artery Disease Detection from Non-Invasive Biomarkers
papers.abstract: We apply XGBoost to 42 non-invasive biomarkers including high-sensitivity troponin, BNP, and carotid IMT from 2,400 patients in a prospective study. AUC 0.88 for obstructive CAD diagnosis (>50% stenosis on angiography) is achieved, outperforming Diamond-Forrester and Duke Clinical Scores. SHAP feature importance confirms troponin and IMT as primary predictors.
papers.topic_hint: XGBoost, CAD detection, non-invasive biomarkers, troponin, IMT
papers.methodology_hint: XGBoost, 2400 patients, AUC 0.88, 2 clinical score baselines, SHAP feature importance
papers.claimed_contribution: XGBoost for CAD detection from biomarkers AUC 0.88, outperforming clinical scores, SHAP analysis.

papers.title: Federated CNN for Arrhythmia Classification Across 20 Hospital ECG Networks
papers.abstract: We apply federated learning to train an arrhythmia classification model across 20 hospitals without sharing patient ECGs. The federated model achieves AUC 0.91, within 3% of centralised training. We note this is a federated training methodology paper; the diagnostic model uses a standard published architecture.
papers.topic_hint: federated learning, ECG, arrhythmia classification, multi-site
papers.methodology_hint: federated learning, 20 hospitals, AUC 0.91, 3% below centralised — methodology paper
papers.claimed_contribution: Federated arrhythmia classification AUC 0.91 across 20 hospitals, methodology paper.

papers.title: Meta-Analysis of Deep Learning for ECG Interpretation: 112 Studies
papers.abstract: We meta-analyse 112 studies on deep learning for ECG interpretation covering arrhythmia detection, MI diagnosis, and channelopathy classification. Pooled AUC 0.93 for binary tasks is reported. Key methodological concerns include publication bias and lack of external validation. No new model is developed.
papers.topic_hint: meta-analysis, deep learning, ECG, systematic review, 112 studies
papers.methodology_hint: meta-analysis, 112 studies, no new model
papers.claimed_contribution: Meta-analysis of 112 DL-ECG studies, pooled AUC 0.93, methodological quality analysis.

papers.title: Lightweight Transformer for Implantable Device VT/VF Detection with 22% Fewer Shocks
papers.abstract: We develop a lightweight transformer (8 KB, 2 ms inference) for real-time VT/VF detection from implantable cardiac device electrograms. Tested on 18,000 arrhythmia episodes, sensitivity 0.96 and specificity 0.93 are achieved, reducing inappropriate shocks by 22% versus device manufacturer algorithms.
papers.topic_hint: lightweight transformer, VT/VF detection, implantable device, electrogram
papers.methodology_hint: lightweight transformer, 18k episodes, sensitivity 0.96, manufacturer baseline, shock reduction 22%
papers.claimed_contribution: Lightweight transformer for implantable device VT/VF detection, sensitivity 0.96, 22% shock reduction.

papers.title: Causal Inference for Modifiable Heart Failure Risk Factor Identification from EHR
papers.abstract: We apply doubly-robust causal inference to 180,000 patient EHR records to identify causal risk factors for incident heart failure. Hypertension, obesity, and sleep apnoea are confirmed as causal. No predictive or diagnostic ML model is developed.
papers.topic_hint: causal inference, heart failure risk factors, EHR, observational study
papers.methodology_hint: doubly-robust causal inference, 180k patients, risk factors — causal analysis not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: Causal identification of modifiable heart failure risk factors, 180k EHR, no diagnostic model.

papers.title: Graph Transformer for Multi-Scale Cardiac Segmentation Supporting Functional Analysis
papers.abstract: We construct cardiac mesh graphs from CMR and train a GNN to predict six functional phenotypes including ejection fraction and strain. EF MAE 2.1% is achieved. Functional phenotyping supports monitoring but does not perform diagnostic classification of cardiac conditions.
papers.topic_hint: GNN, cardiac mesh, CMR, functional phenotyping, ejection fraction
papers.methodology_hint: GNN, cardiac mesh, EF MAE 2.1% — phenotyping not diagnostic classification
papers.claimed_contribution: GNN for cardiac functional phenotyping EF MAE 2.1%, not diagnostic classification.

papers.title: Semi-Supervised Learning for Cardiac MRI Segmentation with Limited Labels
papers.abstract: We apply mean teacher semi-supervised training to cardiac cine MRI using 150 labelled scans for LV segmentation. Dice 0.93 for LV segmentation is achieved. The method supports downstream analysis; it produces segmentation masks not diagnostic labels.
papers.topic_hint: semi-supervised learning, cardiac MRI, LV segmentation, mean teacher
papers.methodology_hint: mean teacher, 150 labelled CMR, Dice 0.93, segmentation not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: Semi-supervised CMR LV segmentation Dice 0.93, functional analysis support, not diagnosis.

papers.title: Sparse Autoencoder for ECG Anomaly Scoring Without Disease-Specific Labels
papers.abstract: We train a sparse autoencoder on 50,000 normal ECG recordings and use reconstruction error as an anomaly score for detecting unseen cardiac pathologies. AUC 0.86 across five pathology classes is achieved without class-specific supervision. This is an anomaly detection approach; specific cardiac condition diagnosis is not performed.
papers.topic_hint: sparse autoencoder, ECG, anomaly detection, unsupervised, cardiac pathology
papers.methodology_hint: sparse autoencoder, 50k normal ECGs, AUC 0.86, anomaly not specific diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: Sparse autoencoder ECG anomaly detection AUC 0.86, unsupervised, no disease-specific diagnosis.

papers.title: Survey of Deep Learning for Cardiac MRI: Methods and Clinical Applications
papers.abstract: We survey 180 papers on deep learning for cardiac MRI covering segmentation, reconstruction, disease classification, and functional analysis. Benchmark comparisons are provided for key tasks. No new model is proposed.
papers.topic_hint: survey, deep learning, cardiac MRI, 180 papers
papers.methodology_hint: survey, 180 papers, no new model
papers.claimed_contribution: Survey of 180 DL cardiac MRI papers, no new model or evaluation.

papers.title: Wearable ECG Patch for Long-Term Paroxysmal AF Monitoring with Transformer Classifier
papers.abstract: Paroxysmal AF is intermittent and missed by short recordings. We deploy a transformer classifier on a 14-day wearable ECG patch worn by 3,400 cryptogenic stroke patients, detecting AF in 12.3% versus 4.1% for 24-hour Holter. AUC 0.94 for AF episode detection is achieved with real-world external validation.
papers.topic_hint: wearable ECG, paroxysmal AF, long-term monitoring, transformer, cryptogenic stroke
papers.methodology_hint: transformer, 14-day wearable, 3400 patients, AUC 0.94, AF detection, real-world validation
papers.claimed_contribution: Transformer on 14-day wearable ECG for AF detection AUC 0.94, real-world validation in stroke patients.
